brief.objective: invite teams to strengthen alignment conversations
keyMessages: WebbAlign helps teams use the lens of DOK to evaluate standards, learning objectives, assessments, curricula, and instructional materials.
WebbAlign professional learning can help teams build a calibrated understanding of DOK to inform practice.
DOK supports conversations about cognitive complexity and coherent learning opportunities.
proofPoints: The WebbAlign homepage identifies WebbAlign as a WCEPS program.
The WebbAlign site states that the team works in collaboration with Dr. Norman Webb.
The WebbAlign DOK page says DOK differentiates complexity from difficulty.
brief.layoutRecommendation: Use a text-light social graphic with one headline, one approved phrase, generous whitespace, and a caption pack.

headlineOptions: Use DOK to strengthen alignment conversations
Make cognitive complexity part of the curriculum conversation
Look across standards, assessments, and materials
subheadOptions: WebbAlign helps curriculum teams use Depth of Knowledge to evaluate standards, learning objectives, assessments, curricula, and instructional materials.
Structure rich conversations about cognitive complexity through the lens of DOK.
Build a calibrated understanding of DOK to inform alignment work across curriculum and assessment materials.
copy.body: WebbAlign helps curriculum teams use the lens of Depth of Knowledge to evaluate standards, learning objectives, assessments, curricula, and instructional materials. Through professional learning and analysis, teams can build a calibrated understanding of DOK, differentiate complexity from difficulty, and support conversations about coherent learning opportunities.
bullets: Use DOK to examine cognitive complexity across standards and learning objectives.
Review assessments, curricula, and instructional materials through a shared DOK lens.
Differentiate complexity from difficulty when discussing academic expectations and tasks.
Build a calibrated understanding of DOK for alignment conversations.
Work toward an aligned, or coherent, system.

fittedCopy.headline: Use DOK to strengthen alignment conversations
fittedCopy.deck: Curriculum teams use Depth of Knowledge to evaluate standards, learning objectives, assessments.
proofPoints: Use DOK to examine cognitive complexity across standards.
Review assessments, curricula, and instructional materials through.

compositionTemplate.typographyScale: bold social headline with one support line
compositionTemplate.artPlateTreatment: full-square art plate with tonal gradient overlay

designRecipe.hierarchy: bold social headline as the clear hero, concise support line beneath, and a compact CTA anchored bottom-left
designRecipe.artDirection: 1:1 WebbAlign education-leadership poster concept: abstract alignment grid, curriculum pathway lines, and layered assessment/standards cues in a refined blue-green tonal system with calm authority and strong center/lower contrast
designRecipe.placeholderStrategy: create intentional negative space and tonal quiet zones for app-rendered copy; imply structure through depth, gradients, and alignment motifs without fake text, logos, boxes, or UI modules
designRecipe.appComposition: app places the official compact brand mark top-left, renders the center/lower editorial text lockup, adds the bottom-left CTA chip, and handles final export
promptDirectives: Design a complete square social poster concept for curriculum teams discussing DOK and alignment.
Use abstract standards-to-assessments alignment visuals, not literal classroom stock imagery.
Preserve a strong center/lower quiet zone for the app-rendered headline and support line.
Do not generate fake typography, logos, placeholder cards, contact bars, or UI elements.

issues: A fitted proof point ends with a dangling preposition and is not a complete sentence, creating production copy that should block export if visible or retained.
warnings: Render/layout QA reported pass-level results while proofPointCount was nonzero and one proof point was syntactically invalid.
The art plate includes blurred report/chart-like textures that are mostly acceptable but could be perceived as fake data if they become too prominent in final composition.

safeZones: Keep important visual detail away from the outer 96px crop edge.
Use a strong central subject or visual rhythm that supports a social headline.
Avoid tiny details that collapse at feed size.

Curriculum teams use Depth of Knowledge to evaluate standards, learning
Use DOK to examine cognitive complexity across standards.
appOwnedElements: Exact headline, audience, CTA, and review state.
imageGenOwnedElements: Premium abstract or editorial education-sector atmosphere.
Color, texture, photography or illustration, and crop energy only.

imageResults.revisedPrompt:
Create a text-free 1024x1024 square social campaign art plate for WebbAlign, intended for curriculum teams, with a precise professional systems-oriented tone. Edge-to-edge premium education-sector poster concept, not a flyer, brochure, webpage, contact card, or UI. No readable text, no letters, no numbers, no labels, no fake paragraphs, no logos, no wordmarks, no initials, no badges, no seals, no icons with labels, no arrows, no chevrons, no mountain-like marks.

Visual concept: abstract standards-to-assessments-to-curriculum alignment expressed through layered depth, calibrated grid structures, translucent pathway lines, nested matrices, and subtle assessment/curriculum material cues. Use refined abstract-illustrative atmosphere with hints of editorial photography: blurred standards documents and curriculum maps as texture only, assessment packets with completely unreadable marks, workshop-table analytical energy without close-up faces, detailed hands, or posed stock-photo people. Implied DOK/calibration seriousness through structured depth and measured alignment cues, but no fake diagrams or labels.

Composition: full designed 1:1 social poster art plate with bold color movement and strong visual hierarchy. Keep official app-rendered brand mark area clear at top-left; reserve a strong center/lower quiet zone for app-rendered headline and support copy with tonal contrast and intentional negative space, but do not draw boxes or panels. Compact CTA will be app-rendered bottom-left, so keep that corner calm and readable without creating a contact bar. Important visual detail should stay away from the outer 96px crop. Use central visual rhythm: layered alignment grid and curriculum pathway lines sweeping from upper-right/background toward mid-center, with quieter deep navy/teal gradient in center-lower area.

Palette: deep navy #142836, teal #0081A4, aqua #3EB3BD, warm accent #F7D470. Premium gradients, subtle grain, glassy translucent layers, analytical light, crisp but not cluttered. Minimal density, social-feed legibility, NY media/ad-agency quality. Avoid generic classroom/hallway, literal charts, readable document layouts, placeholder modules, cards, mock UI, template scaffolding, compliance-approval implication, endorsement imagery, and stock-photo workshop cliches.

logoAsset.label: Horizontal full color · transparent · light/image backgrounds

request.goal: invite teams to strengthen alignment conversations

sourceEvidence.excerpt: Learn more about how we can help you to effectively use DOK to promote alignment.
sourceEvidence.label: WebbAlign homepage positioning

sourceEvidence.excerpt: Use the lens of DOK to evaluate standards, learning objectives, assessments, curricula, and instructional materials.
sourceEvidence.label: WebbAlign evaluation areas

sourceEvidence.excerpt: DOK supports educators, districts, assessment developers, states and others to evaluate and improve assessments and inform alignment.
sourceEvidence.label: DOK and coherent systems

sourceEvidence.excerpt: Structuring conversations about cognitive complexity through the lens of DOK leads to rich conversations.
sourceEvidence.label: Curriculum team conversations
